title: Stocks Fall on Iran Jitters, Intel Delivers Earnings Beat | The Close 4/23/2026
snippet: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Creditsights’ Zachary Griffiths, McKinsey’s K…
contentText: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Creditsights’ Zachary Griffiths, McKinsey’s Kate Smaje, Jefferies’ John Hecht, Raymond James CEO Paul Shoukry, Zscaler CEO Jay Chaudhry, Janus Henderson Investors’ Michael Contopoulos, CFRA Research’s Angelo Zino, Great Hill Capital’s Thomas Hayes, PIMCO’s Libby Cantrill, Stifel CEO Ron Kruszewski, & Former NFL Network & ESPN CEO Steve Bornstein. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Why Anthropic’s Mythos Is Sparking Alarm
snippet: Mythos has sparked fear as AI may be advancing faster than it can be safely deployed. But the furor may benefit Anthropic, the model’s owner, as it sees potential valuations approaching $800 billion. (Source: Bloomberg)
contentText: Mythos has sparked fear as AI may be advancing faster than it can be safely deployed. But the furor may benefit Anthropic, the model’s owner, as it sees potential valuations approaching $800 billion. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SoftBank Prepares to Manufacture Batteries for AI Data Centers
snippet: SoftBank Group Corp.’s mobile unit plans to transform part of its factory in Osaka into one of Japan’s biggest production lines for large-scale batteries in an ambitious attempt at powering its own AI data centers.
contentText: SoftBank Group Corp.’s mobile unit plans to transform part of its factory in Osaka into one of Japan’s biggest production lines for large-scale batteries in an ambitious attempt at powering its own AI data centers.

title: Bezos’ Physical AI Lab Has Closed Round at $38 Billion Value
snippet: Project Prometheus, the artificial intelligence lab that Amazon.com Inc. founder Jeff Bezos is leading with Google veteran Vik Bajaj, has closed a $10 billion funding round that values the company at roughly $38 billion…
contentText: Project Prometheus, the artificial intelligence lab that Amazon.com Inc. founder Jeff Bezos is leading with Google veteran Vik Bajaj, has closed a $10 billion funding round that values the company at roughly $38 billion, according to a person familiar with the situation.

title: An AI Agent Takes Over a Store and Orders Too Many Candles
snippet: In San Francisco’s upscale Cow Hollow district, the introduction of a boutique selling coffee table games, tote bags and other household items would be pretty unremarkable. However, Andon Market has one key differentiat…
contentText: In San Francisco’s upscale Cow Hollow district, the introduction of a boutique selling coffee table games, tote bags and other household items would be pretty unremarkable. However, Andon Market has one key differentiator: It’s run by AI.At this store, an artificial intelligence agent named Luna effectively acts as the chief executive officer of the operation. It decides what products to offer and how much to charge for them. Among its picks are the word game Bananagrams and a handful of books a

title: Tesla Plans Additional $25 Billion in Spending | Bloomberg Tech 4/23/2026
snippet: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Tesla’s plans to spend an additional $25 billion this year to support Elon Musk's AI ambitions. Plus, Intel shares jump after the company pledges to support Musk's advance…
contentText: Bloomberg’s Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow discuss Tesla’s plans to spend an additional $25 billion this year to support Elon Musk's AI ambitions. Plus, Intel shares jump after the company pledges to support Musk's advanced chip manufacturing project, Terafab. And, Lyft CEO David Risher discusses the company’s international growth plans as the ride-hailing firm buys the London black cab app Gett. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Blackstone COO | Growth and AI-Driven Economic Tailwinds
snippet: Jon Gray, Blackstone President & COO, addressed investor concerns about market volatility and liquidity pressures. He emphasized that while noise and uncertainty may prompt investors to seek liquidity, the resilience of…
contentText: Jon Gray, Blackstone President & COO, addressed investor concerns about market volatility and liquidity pressures. He emphasized that while noise and uncertainty may prompt investors to seek liquidity, the resilience of certain financial products, particularly Business Development Companies (BDCs), is a key factor to watch. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Tesla Sales Helped by High Gas Prices
snippet: Tesla says rising gas prices have boosted interest in its electric vehicles. The surprisingly optimistic comments came weeks after the automaker reported lower-than-expected car sales to start the year and as it pivots…
contentText: Tesla says rising gas prices have boosted interest in its electric vehicles. The surprisingly optimistic comments came weeks after the automaker reported lower-than-expected car sales to start the year and as it pivots its focus to AI and robotics. Jessica Caldwell, head of insights at Edmunds, joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on "Bloomberg Tech." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: SpaceX Tapped for Group Developing Golden Dome Software
snippet: SpaceX is part of a group of companies developing the operating system underpinning President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome initiative, according to sources. It is an expansion of the role Elon Musk's company is playing in…
contentText: SpaceX is part of a group of companies developing the operating system underpinning President Donald Trump’s Golden Dome initiative, according to sources. It is an expansion of the role Elon Musk's company is playing in the US missile defense shield. Bloomberg’s Michael Shepard joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)

title: YouTuber has DIMM idea, builds working DRAM in backyard
snippet: What are you doing to solve the memory crisis? If you follow PC hardware prices, you’ll know AI demand has pushed memory prices higher as manufacturers prioritize memory for datacenters. To deal with that, you can pay t…
contentText: What are you doing to solve the memory crisis? If you follow PC hardware prices, you’ll know AI demand has pushed memory prices higher as manufacturers prioritize memory for datacenters. To deal with that, you can pay through the nose, buy less memory, or ... try to build your own DRAM.…
